rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling blindsided by a hidden downside in the stoppering investment after approval
rows.imp_rat: Feeling blindsided by a hidden downside after approval matters for the director's sense of security, but it overlaps heavily with the risk-exposure concern and is more emotional than decision-gating.
rows.sat_rat: Risk registers and contingency reserves provide some reassurance, yet hidden qualification or integration downsides in sterile projects still emerge after the capital is committed.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the run
rows.stmt: Diminish the likelihood of feeling overwhelmed when multiple faults occur in quick succession
rows.imp_rat: Feeling overwhelmed by cascading faults hits the operator's sense of control but is secondary to the seal itself.
rows.sat_rat: HMIs stack alarms without prioritization, so multiple simultaneous faults still readily overwhelm an operator.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time required to reconfigure seating parameters when transitioning to a new container format
rows.imp_rat: Reconfiguring seating parameters for a new format is real setup work but occurs between runs, not during the productive core act.
rows.sat_rat: Recipe-driven format recall shortens setup, yet fine seating-height adjustments still need manual verification and dial-in.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of documentation corrections required after a stoppering batch is completed
rows.imp_rat: Post-batch documentation corrections signal control weaknesses and consume QA effort, but they are a compliance-hygiene concern rather than direct sterility.
rows.sat_rat: Electronic records and structured review reduce corrections, though manual stoppering data entries still generate rework.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of erosion in the role's standing as the authoritative voice on repeatable hermetic sealing
rows.imp_rat: Erosion of the role's authority on repeatable hermetic sealing weakens their influence, though it is a status concern secondary to the functional verification job.
rows.sat_rat: Established expertise and documentation generally preserve the role's standing, so significant erosion is not the routine experience today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to assemble supporting financial evidence into a defensible business case for the seating-and-sealing capability
rows.imp_rat: Assembling financial evidence is effortful but a supporting task; slow assembly delays rather than defeats the funding of the sealing capability.
rows.sat_rat: Finance templates and prior capital cases give reusable structure, making evidence assembly moderately efficient today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of setup variation between operators performing the same format changeover
rows.imp_rat: Operator-to-operator setup variation affects consistency, but an individual operator experiences it less acutely than in-run seal issues.
rows.sat_rat: Electronic recipes reduce variation, yet manual seating adjustments still differ noticeably between operators.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of field seal failures that would discredit my standing as the seal-integrity authority
rows.imp_rat: Field seal failures that discredit the engineer's standing carry higher status stakes because they are public and consequential, though still downstream of the technical job.
rows.sat_rat: Field failures are relatively rare under current qualification regimes but not eliminated, leaving moderate residual reputational exposure.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that my seal-integrity standards are second-guessed by suppliers or peers across the organization
rows.imp_rat: Being second-guessed by suppliers or peers is a status concern that matters less than actual seal performance to the engineer's core technical job.
rows.sat_rat: Established compendial and internal standards give the engineer reasonable defensibility today, moderating how often they are second-guessed.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which inconsistent results across the product range erode my recognition as the definitive seal authority
rows.imp_rat: Recognition as the definitive seal authority is a secondary status motive that rides on, but is not central to, doing the core job well.
rows.sat_rat: Inconsistent cross-range results do occasionally dent standing, and current tools only partly prevent such inconsistency.
